seoTitle: Simpson vs Stapleton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Simpson and Stapleton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Simpson and Stapleton.
heading: Simpson vs Stapleton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Simpson, Northern Territory and Stapleton, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stapleton has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Simpson (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Simpson or Stapleton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Simpson has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Stapleton scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Simpson scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Stapleton scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Simpson or Stapleton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stapleton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
